reviews.review_description:
I'm always on the lookout for more Go movement games. With its simple controller it's not as good for this as the Quest, for example, but ever since buying Very Star I'm convinced that the Go is decent enough for such games, so I decided to give Song Beater a try.

I'm a little tired of the Beat Saber aesthetics of monochromatic black and neon. I prefer Very Star's colourful arcs and stages. I wish there was at least an option to switch to something more colourful. The graphics and effects are generally well done, though, discounting their copycat nature.

The game has a decent selection of songs, and it wasn't hard to find something I was willing to play to. There are also 'custom' and 'video' selection tabs, which are empty, and I'm not sure what they are meant to contain. Very Star and Smash the Beats automatically recognised songs in the music directory and generated beats for them (although neither worked well).

I tried the various modes. The only one which I felt gave me real feedback was the axe. The staff blinked on and off so wasn't that playable, the tonfas were just all over the place, the blade felt like it wasn't meant for slicing, just for poking, and the phaser was neat but it wasn't clear to me why I was sometimes missing even though I thought I was aiming right.

So when it comes to control, I prefer Very Star. There I make large moves with my arm, and it feels natural. So I end up moving my arm and body quite a bit. In Song Beater I feel a need to conform to the body model of the controller, where my elbow is in, and that makes all movement quite small. I think that the axe would be nice if I could set it to be farther from the body.

All in all, I like this better than Smash the Beats, because it makes me reach higher and doesn't have annoying bombs which disrupt the songs, but I still enjoy the movement in Very Star more and would continue to play that. Still, I'm not planning to refund, and hope the developer will improve the game over time.

I don't get it how you are supposed to use the staff when it's constantly blinking on and off. There are notes coming to you which you can't hit when the staff goes off. It would be cool if you could use the trigger button to activate the staff and then have like a power bar that depletes by too much using it. Also the blade misses the notes way too often even when you clearly slice them so you end up using it only to stab. Look at how the game Hardlight Blade did this way better in their music mode.

The saw and the fist are the only weapons who work great but are also the most boring ones to play. I would love to play a freestyle mode where I can switch the weapons in the middle of the track but at first there must be put more effort into the controls of them.

For now I can't recommend this game and would rather play Very Star or Beatron 2000.

developer_response.body: We have fixed some controlling issues (staff weapon etc.) in latest update and added new features and content as well. You can check it out and let us know your thoughts, thanks!

reviews.review_description: I took a chance on this game and am really enjoying it.   The only thing that would make it even better is adding the ability to load your own songs.

reviews.review_title: Great game on the Oculus Go
reviews.review_description:
Worth every penny,  cant stop playing this, great for the grand kids and for us grown ups .
Very easy to add your own songs.
And It doesn't take long to find your way around the settings. 
Love it.

The latest update has revived 3D expression!
It ’s a very fun game.
Tried several times to install and I'm told it will not work on my device. Galaxy Note 9+. Please advise.
Finally turned video games into a fun and healthy exercise! Thanks for this so much!

reviews.review_title: My #1 favorite game (and I have a lot)
reviews.review_description:
So I’ve bought like 70+ games at this point? Certain popular games I just know I won’t like even if it’s a great game, I have a good idea of what I like and how I prefer to spend my time in VR, and I’d like to think I have a pretty broad spectrum of all the most popular games and some “hidden gems” at this point. I use my Go daily but I haven’t actually purchased anything new in a couple months now (I went all out and got everything that looked really good to me when COVID started).

I had a few things left on my wish list and looked through the ENTIRE oculus store again since I rarely pay too much attention to it these days. Long story short I got 5 new, solid games, all of which I’m enjoying (a couple left to buy). This game had caught my eye once in the past, and I remember reading about how porting a 6dof game to 3dof can be strange. I admit, there are quite a few things that don’t work quite the way they’re supposed to...but there are so many different game modes and weapons (axe is my go-to). As cool as all the different options are (video playback is crazy), what really makes this game shine are first and foremost the custom tracks which keep the game fresh af...and then between the nice graphics and the couple game modes that do work (and it feels more satisfying than any other Go rhythm games including smash the beats), idk how to even describe it but you can really get into this game. 

Yea it would be awesome to have a game without bugs or features that don’t work properly, but life isn’t fair, and you take what you can get lol. I can honestly say this is the most fun I’ve had playing any Go game, and I expect it to stay fun for a long time to come. For $5 how can you go wrong?
The game is good just need more songs and how do u put custome song on the game

developer_response.body: Hi, you can put custom songs to your device by copying them manually or by using SideQuest application (it support wide range of Android-based VR headsets). See more information here: http://songbeater.com/custom.html

reviews.review_description: Tried this on the Go. Not top notch visuals but a lot of fun to play. I also got an arm and shoulder workout. Recommended
This game is one of the best on Go!! It has custom songs so it never gets old. I only wish is was a way to create songs from the app instead of a computer. Other than that, this is great
I wish they had a tutorial on how to add video in the background . This game is well worth the money! I love the Voice announcer and the atmosphere of the game
I love this game is my #1 fave on The oculus go but I don't know how to add my own songs😢. And also .do I need a pc to upload my own songs cuz I don't have one

Very good game I think it's very precise with the movements and controllers on the Gear.Since Beat Saber probably will never come to the Gear/Go Store this one is a must buy but:

- I would like to know how to add custom songs to it,I saw the modding tab in game and it came to my interests.

-I have some issues trying to go back to Oculus Home,when I hard press the back button the screen goes black and I have to detach and attach the device again, it's bothersome.

-A YouTube mode with some random beat generation would be good to pass the time as a new mode to play and the ability to choose your own music from the storage, won't need to be perfect just need to be there.

This plays a sound so you know if you hit the target, which is noticeably missing from the smash beats game. It has plenty of songs and modes to choose from, and a multiplayer lobby. Buy this over other alternatives to Beat Saber.

Fun once you figure out how to hit. It was super frustrating without a tutorial, so you have to fail over and over trying to figure out the way you need to hit by trial and error. (Hint - let them get real close to you, and don’t switch hands if one gets tired, it doesn’t work with your other hand) But it’s fun once you get the hang of it. Still no idea how to upload your own stuff, since again there are NO instructions.
Love it wish I could figure out how to use two controllers
